meta.purpose: Reduced, structured research findings for rigging GameLoop's demo to the real weekend of 2026-07-24 to 2026-07-26 in Toronto. Not raw API dumps or scraped HTML. Shaped for easy transformation into app fixtures, loosely following lib/data/venue.json's field-naming conventions (id, name, source, priceCad-style fields, dietaryFlags).
meta.unionStationAddress: 65 Front St W, Toronto, ON M5J 1E6
meta.dietaryEnumNote: Task scoped research to gluten-free, halal, vegetarian, nut-free. Actual lib/planning/schemas.ts DietaryNeedSchema has 6 values: gluten-free, vegetarian, vegan, nut-free, dairy-free, halal.

events.name: Tame Impala: The Deadbeat Tour (with Djo)
events.notes: Strongest real, walkable anchor for the target weekend. Doors time not published in any source checked.

events.name: Tame Impala: The Deadbeat Tour (with Djo)
events.notes: Second night of the two-night stand.

events.name: No Scotiabank Arena event found
events.notes: Negative finding, only official listing and one aggregator checked.

events.name: NHL offseason confirmation
events.notes: 2026-27 regular season opens 2026-09-29; preseason 2026-09-19 to 2026-09-26. FLAG: NHL.com coverage refers to Carolina as 'defending Stanley Cup champion', which conflicts with the app's existing showcase-game-a.json (Vegas Golden Knights won 5-4 in 2OT, 2026-06-06). Not adjudicated in this research; outside scope.

events.name: Toronto Blue Jays away at Boston Red Sox (Fenway Park)
events.notes: Fri Jul 24 7:15pm ET, Sat Jul 25 4:10pm ET, Sun Jul 26 1:35pm ET, all at Fenway Park. No Blue Jays home games this weekend; next home game 2026-07-31 vs St. Louis Cardinals 19:07.

events.name: FIFA World Cup 2026 match (Portugal vs Croatia, Round of 32)
events.notes: Outside target weekend, included for context only. BMO Field hosted World Cup matches June 12 to July 2, 2026.

events.name: Toronto FC (MLS) paused for World Cup, no July home games
events.notes: MLS paused May 25 to July 16, 2026 for World Cup; TFC home stretch resumes August 2026.

events.name: Toronto Argonauts (CFL) away, no BMO Field home game that weekend
events.notes: Argos at BC Place (Vancouver) vs BC Lions on 2026-07-25. First home game back at BMO Field after World Cup disruption is 2026-08-06 vs Calgary.

restaurants.notes: Closes 19:00 both weekend days, fine for pre-game only.

restaurants.name: Real Sports Bar & Grill

rating.reviewCountNote: 9133 reviews; a separate Yelp listing at the same address shows 575 reviews

glutenFree.note: Named GF items exist (salmon poke bowl, garlic fingers) but at least one allergy-tracking source states this is not a dedicated GF facility, cross-contact risk from fryers.

restaurants.notes: Highest review volume of any candidate; the iconic no-brainer pre-game anchor given direct arena attachment.

restaurants.notes: GAP EXAMPLE: closed Sunday entirely, one of two target dates unusable.

constructionForTargetWeekend.note: No disruption found for 2026-07-25/26 on the line's own construction notices page; next listed disruption starts 2026-09-26. Corroborated by a separate service-alert search that surfaced disruptions on Barrie, Lakeshore West, UP Express, and Kitchener lines for that weekend but never mentioned Lakeshore East.

unionArrivals1630to1930.note: Schedule tool (transsee.ca) ignored requested date parameter and returned a Monday 2026-07-20 weekday schedule both times queried. Not usable as a Saturday schedule. Recommend pulling the actual GO Transit Saturday timetable directly at build time.
unionArrivals1630to1930.existingAppSnapshotCaveat: lib/data/transit-snapshot.json is explicitly a Tuesday 2026-07-14 weekday snapshot per its own notes field; using it unmodified for a Saturday 'tonight' is a real day-type mismatch.

constructionForTargetWeekend.reason: Planned construction at Exhibition Station for the future Ontario Line subway
specificShifts: normal 08:47 westbound Union departure leaves 2 minutes earlier
westbound trains between 09:17 and 00:17 shift 2 minutes earlier
one late-night train skips Exhibition GO entirely

normalWeekendFrequency.note: 15-minute weekend daytime/evening service between Oakville GO and Union restored starting 2026-05-02 (announced 2026-04-14); hourly base West Harbour-Union; ~30 minutes further out normally. Unclear whether the July 25-26 'every 30 minutes' figure is a temporary drop from 15 minutes or the unaffected outer-line frequency.

unionArrivals1630to1930.note: Same schedule-tool limitation as Lakeshore East; not usable as a Saturday schedule.

constructionForTargetWeekend.detail: UP Express trains replaced by GO buses on 2026-07-25 and 2026-07-26 for planned construction; no UP Express service at Bloor, Mount Dennis, and Weston stations during the closure.

normalWeekendFrequency.value: every 15 minutes, first train 06:00, last train 01:00
normalWeekendFrequency.note: Sourced figure traced to a Metrolinx PDF labeled for a different June 2026 event window; moot for the target weekend regardless since it is under construction disruption.

upExpress.appFixtureRelevance: lib/data/transit-snapshot.json only models Lakeshore East/West GO trains, not UP Express, so this is informational only.

certificationBodiesOntario.name: Islamic Food and Nutrition Council of Canada (IFANCC)

halal.regulatoryNote: Certifying organizations are not government-regulated in Canada; 'halal certified' is only as trustworthy as the named certifier.
threeTierLanguage: certified halal (named, checkable certifier on record for the exact outlet)
self-described halal (restaurant's own claim, no named certifier)
halal-friendly / halal options available (partial menu or soft language)

glutenFree.distinction: celiac-safe (dedicated GF kitchen, separate fryers/prep/utensils, trained staff) vs gluten-free options (GF ingredients in a shared kitchen that also handles wheat/barley/rye, trace cross-contact possible)
glutenFree.crossContactRiskNote: Roughly a third of restaurant dishes labeled gluten-free in one crowd-sourced study still tested positive for gluten; pizza and pasta are the highest-risk categories.
glutenFree.ontarioRegulatoryNote: Ontario has no mandatory restaurant menu allergen-disclosure law found in this research, unlike packaged retail food under federal Health Canada priority-allergen rules.

vegetarian.distinction: fully vegetarian/vegan menu by concept (no cross-contact category) vs vegetarian/vegan options within an omnivore menu (shared kitchen, cross-contact possible)

nutFree.finding: Thinnest category researched; no findable, citable nut-allergy policy for any of the 15 candidate restaurants.
nutFree.generalPattern: Only about 16 percent of a sampled set of Toronto restaurant menus carried any allergy disclaimer at all in one academic source; where present, disclaimers are almost always a generic cross-contact statement, not nut-specific.
nutFree.recommendation: Do not print a nut-free badge for any real restaurant in this candidate list. Reuse the app's existing lib/copy.ts dietaryDisclaimer pattern ('Cross-contact information is unavailable; confirm with venue staff.') for real restaurants too.
